Century Health — Real World Evidence: Lupus Patient Analysis

Data Analyst Take-Home

2026-03-01

0. Setup & Data Loading

library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(readxl)
library(scales)
library(patchwork)
library(broom)
library(testthat)
library(knitr)

# Load modular helper functions
source("R/helpers.R")

cat("All libraries and helpers loaded.\n")
## All libraries and helpers loaded.
raw <- load_all_datasets("data")

cat(sprintf("Patients:    %d x %d\n", nrow(raw$patients),    ncol(raw$patients)))
## Patients:    10000 x 27
cat(sprintf("Encounters:  %d x %d\n", nrow(raw$encounters),  ncol(raw$encounters)))
## Encounters:  104262 x 15
cat(sprintf("Symptoms:    %d x %d\n", nrow(raw$symptoms),    ncol(raw$symptoms)))
## Symptoms:    1423 x 9
cat(sprintf("Medications: %d x %d\n", nrow(raw$medications), ncol(raw$medications)))
## Medications: 22237 x 13
cat(sprintf("Conditions:  %d x %d\n", nrow(raw$conditions),  ncol(raw$conditions)))
## Conditions:  4149 x 6

1. Data Assessment

1.1 Entity-Relationship Diagram

The five datasets are connected through patient UUIDs and encounter UUIDs:

                          ┌──────────────┐
                          │   PATIENTS   │
                          │ (PATIENT_ID) │
                          └──────┬───────┘
                                 │  1
              ┌──────────────────┼──────────────────┐
              │                  │                  │
              ▼ *                ▼ *                ▼ *
     ┌────────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐
     │  ENCOUNTERS    │  │  SYMPTOMS    │  │  MEDICATIONS │
     │  (Id)          │  │  (PATIENT)   │  │  (PATIENT,   │
     │  FK: PATIENT   │  │              │  │   ENCOUNTER) │
     └───────┬────────┘  └──────────────┘  └──────┬───────┘
             │ 1                                  │
             │            ┌──────────────┐        │
             └───────────►│  CONDITIONS  │◄───────┘
                     *    │  (PATIENT,   │    *
                          │   ENCOUNTER) │
                          └──────────────┘
From To Join Key
Patients → Encounters PATIENT_ID = PATIENT
Patients → Symptoms PATIENT_ID = PATIENT
Patients → Medications PATIENT_ID = PATIENT
Patients → Conditions PATIENT_ID = PATIENT
Encounters → Medications Id = ENCOUNTER
Encounters → Conditions Id = ENCOUNTER

Note on file formats: The raw encounters data is provided as Parquet. Because the R arrow package is not always available, a pre-converted encounters.csv is included in the submission. See R/helpers.R for regeneration instructions.

1.2 Key Data Quality Issues Identified

# Issue Impact Resolution
1 Mixed-case UUID keys across files Joins fail silently Normalise all IDs to lowercase
2 Same medication in 3+ casing variants Inflated distinct-med counts Standardise to uppercase
3 GENDER column entirely NA in symptoms Missing demographic info Backfill from patients table
4 Date columns stored as strings Can’t do time-based analysis Parse to Date / POSIXct
5 SYMPTOMS is a packed key:value string Not analysable directly Parse into 4 numeric columns
6 Symptoms table has duplicate rows per patient Overstates sample size, mixes pathologies Filter to Lupus pathology, deduplicate to 1 row/patient
7 File format heterogeneity (CSV, Parquet, XLSX) Loading complexity Handled in load_all_datasets()

1.3 Two Cohort Definitions

The data supports two natural cohort definitions for Lupus patients. We label them clearly since they have different sizes and are used in different parts of this analysis:

Cohort Definition Size
Diagnosed cohort Patients with at least one Lupus erythematosus record in conditions 2,784
Symptom-observed subset Diagnosed patients who also have a Lupus row in symptoms 948

The symptom-observed subset is a strict subset of the diagnosed cohort. Only this subset has individual symptom scores, so it is the basis for Sections 3.4, 4, and 5. Sections 3.1–3.3 report on the full diagnosed cohort.

2. Data Cleaning

# Step 1: Normalise IDs to lowercase
datasets <- normalise_ids(raw)

# Step 2: Standardise medication names
datasets$medications <- standardise_medication_names(datasets$medications)
cat(sprintf("Medication descriptions: %d unique (after normalisation)\n",
            n_distinct(datasets$medications$DESCRIPTION)))
## Medication descriptions: 5 unique (after normalisation)
# Step 3: Parse dates (including DEATHDATE and conditions$STOP)
datasets <- parse_dates(datasets)

# Step 4: Parse symptom scores
datasets$symptoms <- parse_symptom_scores(datasets$symptoms)

# Step 5: Filter to Lupus-only rows and deduplicate to one row per patient
cat(sprintf("Symptoms before Lupus filter: %d rows, %d unique patients\n",
            nrow(datasets$symptoms), n_distinct(datasets$symptoms$PATIENT)))
## Symptoms before Lupus filter: 1423 rows, 948 unique patients
datasets$symptoms <- filter_lupus_symptoms(datasets$symptoms)
cat(sprintf("Symptoms after  Lupus filter: %d rows, %d unique patients\n",
            nrow(datasets$symptoms), n_distinct(datasets$symptoms$PATIENT)))
## Symptoms after  Lupus filter: 948 rows, 948 unique patients
# Step 6: Fill missing GENDER in symptoms from patients table
datasets$symptoms <- fill_symptom_gender(datasets$symptoms, datasets$patients)
cat(sprintf("Symptom GENDER nulls remaining: %d\n",
            sum(is.na(datasets$symptoms$GENDER))))
## Symptom GENDER nulls remaining: 0
# Show parsed symptom scores
datasets$symptoms %>%
  select(PATIENT, PATHOLOGY, Rash, Joint_Pain, Fatigue, Fever) %>%
  head(5) %>%
  kable(caption = "Parsed Lupus symptom scores (first 5 rows)")
Parsed Lupus symptom scores (first 5 rows)
PATIENT PATHOLOGY Rash Joint_Pain Fatigue Fever
28d7b56c-6056-d0a2-2991-39d6e917216c Lupus erythematosus 34 39 9 12
6c434506-fb4b-3e3f-c19d-553dec3b6c17 Lupus erythematosus 19 44 48 15
44a8ca45-6c6e-38bb-fac0-ddbf7a7ee3a4 Lupus erythematosus 2 32 12 6
780ec78c-22a0-fcdb-17c6-ae9b2fcace9c Lupus erythematosus 30 30 41 19
cf5956bb-34f2-841b-2505-57b99991c377 Lupus erythematosus 28 26 33 5

2.1 Data Integrity Unit Tests

run_integrity_tests(datasets)

Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed

cat("\n**All data integrity tests passed.**\n")

All data integrity tests passed.

# Extract cleaned tables for downstream analysis
patients    <- datasets$patients
encounters  <- datasets$encounters
symptoms    <- datasets$symptoms
medications <- datasets$medications
conditions  <- datasets$conditions

# ── Define the two cohorts ──
# Diagnosed cohort: all patients with a Lupus condition record
lupus_patient_ids <- unique(conditions %>%
  filter(str_to_upper(DESCRIPTION) == "LUPUS ERYTHEMATOSUS") %>%
  pull(PATIENT))

lupus_patients <- patients %>% filter(PATIENT_ID %in% lupus_patient_ids)

# Symptom-observed subset: diagnosed patients who also have symptom data
symptom_patient_ids <- unique(symptoms$PATIENT)

cat(sprintf("Total patients in file:       %s\n", comma(n_distinct(patients$PATIENT_ID))))
## Total patients in file:       10,000
cat(sprintf("Diagnosed Lupus cohort:       %s\n", comma(n_distinct(lupus_patients$PATIENT_ID))))
## Diagnosed Lupus cohort:       2,784
cat(sprintf("Symptom-observed Lupus subset: %s\n", comma(n_distinct(symptom_patient_ids))))
## Symptom-observed Lupus subset: 948

3. Descriptive Analysis

3.1 Distinct Lupus Patients (Diagnosed Cohort)

n_diagnosed <- n_distinct(lupus_patients$PATIENT_ID)
cat(sprintf("Distinct patients diagnosed with Lupus: %s\n", comma(n_diagnosed)))
## Distinct patients diagnosed with Lupus: 2,784

3.2 Distinct Medications Over Time (Diagnosed Cohort)

# Medications for all diagnosed Lupus patients
lupus_meds <- medications %>% filter(PATIENT %in% lupus_patient_ids)

meds_over_time <- lupus_meds %>%
  mutate(YEAR_MONTH = floor_date(START, "month")) %>%
  group_by(YEAR_MONTH) %>%
  summarise(distinct_meds = n_distinct(DESCRIPTION), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(meds_over_time, aes(x = YEAR_MONTH, y = distinct_meds)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, colour = "#2c7fb8") +
  geom_point(size = 1.5, colour = "#2c7fb8") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks()) +
  labs(
    title = "Distinct Medications Prescribed per Month (Diagnosed Lupus Cohort)",
    x     = "Date",
    y     = "Distinct Medications"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

3.3 Diagnosed Cohort Distribution by Race and Gender

# Race pie — diagnosed Lupus cohort
race_data <- lupus_patients %>%
  count(RACE) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n),
         label = sprintf("%s\n%.1f%%", RACE, pct * 100))

p_race <- ggplot(race_data, aes(x = "", y = n, fill = RACE)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, colour = "white") +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  geom_text(aes(label = label),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Diagnosed Lupus Patients by Race", fill = "Race") +
  theme_void()

# Gender pie — diagnosed Lupus cohort
gender_data <- lupus_patients %>%
  count(GENDER) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n),
         label = sprintf("%s\n%.1f%%", GENDER, pct * 100))

p_gender <- ggplot(gender_data, aes(x = "", y = n, fill = GENDER)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, colour = "white") +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  geom_text(aes(label = label),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3.5) +
  labs(title = "Diagnosed Lupus Patients by Gender", fill = "Gender") +
  theme_void()

p_race + p_gender

3.4 Patients with All Four Symptom Categories >= 30 (Symptom-Observed Subset)

# symptoms is one row per patient in the symptom-observed subset
all_high <- symptoms %>%
  filter(Rash >= 30, Joint_Pain >= 30, Fatigue >= 30, Fever >= 30)

pct_high <- nrow(all_high) / nrow(symptoms) * 100

cat(sprintf(
  "Symptom-observed patients with ALL four scores >= 30: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
  nrow(all_high), nrow(symptoms), pct_high
))
## Symptom-observed patients with ALL four scores >= 30: 0 / 948 (0.0%)
cat("(Note: this uses the symptom-observed subset of 948 patients, not the full diagnosed cohort.)\n")
## (Note: this uses the symptom-observed subset of 948 patients, not the full diagnosed cohort.)

4. Cohort Analysis — Treatments, Demographics & Outcomes

Cohort scope: This section uses the symptom-observed subset (948 patients) since symptom scores are required for outcome measurement.

Analysis Plan

We examine how medication type, ethnicity, and age group relate to symptom severity.

Cohort definitions:

Dimension Groups
Ethnicity Hispanic, Non-Hispanic
Age Group Young (<30), Middle (30–54), Older (55+)
Medication Determined by each patient’s most-prescribed therapy (see below)

Outcome measure: Mean composite symptom score (mean of Rash, Joint Pain, Fatigue, Fever).

Approach:

  1. Classify each medication into a therapeutic group.
  2. Identify each patient’s primary therapy (most frequently prescribed; ties broken alphabetically by therapy name).
  3. Report which therapies actually appear as primary in this subset.
  4. Assign age groups and compute composite symptom scores.
  5. Compare outcomes across therapy × ethnicity × age group.
# Classify therapies (all Lupus medications)
lupus_meds <- lupus_meds %>%
  mutate(THERAPY = classify_therapy(DESCRIPTION))

cat("Therapy distribution (all Lupus cohort prescriptions):\n")
## Therapy distribution (all Lupus cohort prescriptions):
lupus_meds %>% count(THERAPY, sort = TRUE) %>% kable()
THERAPY n
Prednisone (Corticosteroid) 10046
Cyclosporine (Immunosuppressant) 9094
Naproxen (NSAID) 2784
Vitamin B12 167
Other 146 
# Restrict medications to the symptom-observed subset
sym_meds <- lupus_meds %>% filter(PATIENT %in% symptom_patient_ids)

# Each patient's primary therapy — deterministic tie-breaking:
#   1. Most prescriptions wins
#   2. Ties broken alphabetically by therapy name
patient_therapy <- sym_meds %>%
  count(PATIENT, THERAPY, name = "n_prescriptions") %>%
  arrange(PATIENT, desc(n_prescriptions), THERAPY) %>%
  group_by(PATIENT) %>%
  slice_head(n = 1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(PATIENT, THERAPY)

# Report which therapies actually survived as primary
cat("Primary therapy distribution (symptom-observed subset):\n")
## Primary therapy distribution (symptom-observed subset):
patient_therapy %>% count(THERAPY, sort = TRUE) %>% kable()
THERAPY n
Prednisone (Corticosteroid) 666
Naproxen (NSAID) 282 
# Add composite score and age group to the deduplicated symptoms table
symptoms <- symptoms %>%
  add_composite_score() %>%
  mutate(AGE_GROUP = assign_age_group(AGE_BEGIN))

# Join symptoms with primary therapy
cohort <- symptoms %>%
  inner_join(patient_therapy, by = "PATIENT")

# Identify which therapies have enough patients for meaningful comparison
therapy_counts <- cohort %>% count(THERAPY, sort = TRUE)
included_therapies <- therapy_counts %>% filter(n >= 10) %>% pull(THERAPY)

cohort_main <- cohort %>% filter(THERAPY %in% included_therapies)

cat(sprintf("\nAnalysis cohort: %d patients (one row each)\n", nrow(cohort_main)))
## 
## Analysis cohort: 948 patients (one row each)
cat("Therapy groups included (n >= 10):\n")
## Therapy groups included (n >= 10):
cohort_main %>% count(THERAPY, sort = TRUE) %>% kable()
THERAPY n
Prednisone (Corticosteroid) 666
Naproxen (NSAID) 282

4.1 Symptom Severity by Therapy and Age Group

summary_age <- cohort_main %>%
  group_by(THERAPY, AGE_GROUP) %>%
  summarise(mean_score = mean(COMPOSITE_SCORE), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(summary_age, aes(x = THERAPY, y = mean_score, fill = AGE_GROUP)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", colour = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Composite Symptom Score by Therapy and Age Group",
    x     = "Therapy",
    y     = "Mean Composite Score",
    fill  = "Age Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 10, hjust = 1))

4.2 Symptom Severity by Therapy and Ethnicity

summary_eth <- cohort_main %>%
  group_by(THERAPY, ETHNICITY) %>%
  summarise(mean_score = mean(COMPOSITE_SCORE), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(summary_eth, aes(x = THERAPY, y = mean_score, fill = ETHNICITY)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", colour = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Composite Symptom Score by Therapy and Ethnicity",
    x     = "Therapy",
    y     = "Mean Composite Score",
    fill  = "Ethnicity"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 10, hjust = 1))

4.3 Heatmap: Therapy × Age Group × Ethnicity

cohort_main <- cohort_main %>%
  mutate(COHORT_LABEL = paste(AGE_GROUP, "/", ETHNICITY))

heat_data <- cohort_main %>%
  group_by(THERAPY, COHORT_LABEL) %>%
  summarise(mean_score = mean(COMPOSITE_SCORE), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(heat_data, aes(x = COHORT_LABEL, y = THERAPY, fill = mean_score)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", mean_score)), size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#ffffcc", high = "#d7191c") +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Composite Symptom Score: Therapy x Cohort",
    x     = "Cohort (Age Group / Ethnicity)",
    y     = "Therapy",
    fill  = "Mean Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))

4.4 Individual Symptom Distributions by Therapy

symptom_long <- cohort_main %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Rash, Joint_Pain, Fatigue, Fever),
               names_to = "Symptom", values_to = "Score")

ggplot(symptom_long, aes(x = THERAPY, y = Score, fill = THERAPY)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Symptom, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Symptom Score Distributions by Therapy",
    x     = NULL,
    y     = "Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom")

4.5 Discussion

# Summarise actual values so the narrative is grounded in outputs
overall_means <- cohort_main %>%
  group_by(THERAPY) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_composite = round(mean(COMPOSITE_SCORE), 1),
    sd_composite   = round(sd(COMPOSITE_SCORE), 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

kable(overall_means,
      col.names = c("Therapy", "N", "Mean Composite", "SD"),
      caption   = "Summary statistics by therapy")
Summary statistics by therapy
Therapy N Mean Composite SD
Naproxen (NSAID) 282 20.7 6.6
Prednisone (Corticosteroid) 666 20.9 6.3 
# Check whether age-group or ethnicity differences are large
age_spread <- summary_age %>%
  group_by(THERAPY) %>%
  summarise(range = round(max(mean_score) - min(mean_score), 1), .groups = "drop")

eth_spread <- summary_eth %>%
  group_by(THERAPY) %>%
  summarise(range = round(max(mean_score) - min(mean_score), 1), .groups = "drop")

cat("\nMax within-therapy difference across age groups:\n")
## 
## Max within-therapy difference across age groups:
print(age_spread)
## # A tibble: 2 x 2
##   THERAPY                     range
##   <chr>                       <dbl>
## 1 Naproxen (NSAID)              2.5
## 2 Prednisone (Corticosteroid)   0.5
cat("\nMax within-therapy difference across ethnicities:\n")
## 
## Max within-therapy difference across ethnicities:
print(eth_spread)
## # A tibble: 2 x 2
##   THERAPY                     range
##   <chr>                       <dbl>
## 1 Naproxen (NSAID)              0.3
## 2 Prednisone (Corticosteroid)   0.2

Observations based on the summaries above:

  • The table above shows the actual mean composite scores and standard deviations for each therapy group that survived primary-therapy assignment. If the means are similar and the SDs overlap, this indicates no observable treatment differentiation.
  • The within-therapy spread across age groups and ethnicities is reported above. Small ranges (a few points) indicate that demographic subgroups do not meaningfully differ within a therapy.
  • The box plots show the full distributions. If the medians and interquartile ranges overlap substantially, this further supports the absence of a treatment-specific signal.
  • These patterns are consistent with Synthea’s simulation approach, which generates symptom scores independently of treatment assignment.

5. Statistical Analysis

Goal

The assignment asks for a model of whether a symptom outcome decreases after a chosen time period following medication. However, the symptom data is cross-sectional — each patient has a single symptom observation, not a before/after pair. There is no longitudinal follow-up in the symptoms table.

Limitation and fallback: Because the data does not support a genuine pre/post comparison, we instead model a cross-sectional outcome: whether Fatigue is below the cohort median (a proxy for relatively low severity). This is a weaker analysis than the assignment requests, but it is the strongest model the data supports without fabricating a temporal structure.

Predictors: therapy type, age, ethnicity, race, gender, total prescription count.

Train/test split: 75/25 random split at the patient level (one row per patient after deduplication, so no leakage risk).

Cohort scope: Symptom-observed subset only.

# Count total prescriptions per patient (within symptom-observed subset)
rx_counts <- sym_meds %>%
  count(PATIENT, name = "N_PRESCRIPTIONS")

# Build modelling dataset
model_df <- cohort_main %>%
  left_join(rx_counts, by = "PATIENT") %>%
  filter(!is.na(GENDER), !is.na(RACE), !is.na(ETHNICITY)) %>%
  mutate(
    LOW_FATIGUE = as.integer(Fatigue < median(Fatigue)),
    THERAPY     = droplevels(factor(THERAPY)),
    GENDER      = droplevels(factor(GENDER)),
    RACE        = droplevels(factor(RACE)),
    ETHNICITY   = droplevels(factor(ETHNICITY))
  )

# Drop factor columns with fewer than 2 levels (can't be used in regression)
single_level_cols <- names(model_df)[sapply(model_df, function(x) {
  is.factor(x) && nlevels(x) < 2
})]
if (length(single_level_cols) > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("Dropping single-level factors: %s\n",
              paste(single_level_cols, collapse = ", ")))
  model_df <- model_df %>% select(-all_of(single_level_cols))
}
## Dropping single-level factors: GENDER
cat(sprintf("Modelling dataset: %d patients\n", nrow(model_df)))
## Modelling dataset: 948 patients
cat(sprintf("Fatigue median threshold: %.0f\n", median(model_df$Fatigue)))
## Fatigue median threshold: 26
cat("Outcome distribution (LOW_FATIGUE):\n")
## Outcome distribution (LOW_FATIGUE):
table(model_df$LOW_FATIGUE) %>% print()
## 
##   0   1 
## 474 474
# Build formula dynamically (skip any dropped single-level factors)
candidate_predictors <- c("AGE_BEGIN", "GENDER", "RACE", "ETHNICITY",
                          "THERAPY", "N_PRESCRIPTIONS")
available_predictors <- intersect(candidate_predictors, names(model_df))
model_formula <- as.formula(
  paste("LOW_FATIGUE ~", paste(available_predictors, collapse = " + "))
)
cat("Model formula:", deparse(model_formula), "\n\n")
## Model formula: LOW_FATIGUE ~ AGE_BEGIN + RACE + ETHNICITY + THERAPY + N_PRESCRIPTIONS
# Fit logistic regression on full dataset for coefficient inspection
logit_fit <- glm(model_formula, data = model_df, family = binomial(link = "logit"))

summary(logit_fit)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = model_formula, family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
##     data = model_df)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                     Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)  
## (Intercept)                         0.805114   0.392218   2.053   0.0401 *
## AGE_BEGIN                          -0.005324   0.004875  -1.092   0.2748  
## RACEblack                          -0.728871   0.319090  -2.284   0.0224 *
## RACEhawaiian                        0.008262   0.809632   0.010   0.9919  
## RACEnative                         -0.362424   0.863935  -0.420   0.6748  
## RACEother                          -1.930614   1.130188  -1.708   0.0876 .
## RACEwhite                          -0.282228   0.275314  -1.025   0.3053  
## ETHNICITYnonhispanic               -0.184964   0.133144  -1.389   0.1648  
## THERAPYPrednisone (Corticosteroid) -0.012643   0.211531  -0.060   0.9523  
## N_PRESCRIPTIONS                    -0.020868   0.034281  -0.609   0.5427  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 1314.2  on 947  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 1300.6  on 938  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 1320.6
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4

5.1 Odds Ratios and Confidence Intervals

or_table <- tidy(logit_fit, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  select(term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value) %>%
  rename(
    `Odds Ratio`     = estimate,
    `CI Lower (95%)` = conf.low,
    `CI Upper (95%)` = conf.high,
    `p-value`        = p.value
  )

kable(or_table, digits = 3, caption = "Logistic Regression — Odds Ratios")
Logistic Regression <U+2014> Odds Ratios
term Odds Ratio CI Lower (95%) CI Upper (95%) p-value
(Intercept) 2.237 1.042 4.864 0.040
AGE_BEGIN 0.995 0.985 1.004 0.275
RACEblack 0.482 0.256 0.898 0.022
RACEhawaiian 1.008 0.204 5.513 0.992
RACEnative 0.696 0.119 4.076 0.675
RACEother 0.145 0.007 0.981 0.088
RACEwhite 0.754 0.436 1.289 0.305
ETHNICITYnonhispanic 0.831 0.640 1.079 0.165
THERAPYPrednisone (Corticosteroid) 0.987 0.652 1.495 0.952
N_PRESCRIPTIONS 0.979 0.916 1.047 0.543

5.2 Model Evaluation

# Random train/test split at patient level (one row per patient)
set.seed(42)
train_idx <- sample(seq_len(nrow(model_df)), size = floor(0.75 * nrow(model_df)))
train_df  <- model_df[train_idx, ]
test_df   <- model_df[-train_idx, ]

cat(sprintf("Train: %d patients, Test: %d patients\n",
            nrow(train_df), nrow(test_df)))
## Train: 711 patients, Test: 237 patients
# Refit on training data
logit_train <- glm(model_formula, data = train_df, family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# Predict on test data
test_df$pred_prob  <- predict(logit_train, newdata = test_df, type = "response")
test_df$pred_class <- as.integer(test_df$pred_prob >= 0.5)

# Confusion matrix
cat("\nConfusion Matrix:\n")
## 
## Confusion Matrix:
table(Predicted = test_df$pred_class, Actual = test_df$LOW_FATIGUE) %>% print()
##          Actual
## Predicted  0  1
##         0 40 30
##         1 87 80
# Accuracy
acc <- mean(test_df$pred_class == test_df$LOW_FATIGUE)
cat(sprintf("\nAccuracy: %.1f%%\n", acc * 100))
## 
## Accuracy: 50.6%
# ROC Curve
roc_data <- tibble(
  truth = test_df$LOW_FATIGUE,
  prob  = test_df$pred_prob
) %>%
  arrange(desc(prob)) %>%
  mutate(
    tpr = cumsum(truth == 1) / sum(truth == 1),
    fpr = cumsum(truth == 0) / sum(truth == 0)
  )

auc_val <- abs(sum(diff(roc_data$fpr) *
  (head(roc_data$tpr, -1) + tail(roc_data$tpr, -1)) / 2))

ggplot(roc_data, aes(x = fpr, y = tpr)) +
  geom_line(colour = "#2c7fb8", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.6, y = 0.3,
           label = sprintf("AUC = %.3f", auc_val), size = 4.5) +
  labs(
    title = "ROC Curve — Low Fatigue Classification",
    x     = "False Positive Rate",
    y     = "True Positive Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

5.3 Model Interpretation

How to read the output:

  • Coefficients (log-odds): Each coefficient represents the change in log-odds of having low Fatigue for a one-unit increase in that predictor, holding all others constant.

  • Odds Ratios: OR > 1 means the predictor increases the odds of low Fatigue relative to the reference category; OR < 1 decreases it.

  • p-values: Values below 0.05 indicate statistical significance at the 5% level.

  • Confidence Intervals: If the 95% CI for an odds ratio crosses 1.0, the association is not statistically significant.

# Dynamically report findings based on actual model output
sig_terms <- or_table %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)", `p-value` < 0.05)
n_sig <- nrow(sig_terms)

cat("**Key findings from this model:**\n\n")

Key findings from this model:

if (n_sig == 0) {
  cat("- No predictor reaches statistical significance at the 5% level.\n")
  cat("- This means therapy type, demographics, and prescription volume are ",
      "not meaningfully associated with Fatigue level in this cohort.\n")
} else {
  cat(sprintf("- %d predictor(s) reached significance at p < 0.05:\n", n_sig))
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(sig_terms))) {
    cat(sprintf("  - **%s**: OR = %.2f (95%% CI: %.2f\u2013%.2f, p = %.3f)\n",
                sig_terms$term[i], sig_terms$`Odds Ratio`[i],
                sig_terms$`CI Lower (95%)`[i], sig_terms$`CI Upper (95%)`[i],
                sig_terms$`p-value`[i]))
  }
}
  • 1 predictor(s) reached significance at p < 0.05:
    • RACEblack: OR = 0.48 (95% CI: 0.26<U+2013>0.90, p = 0.022)
cat(sprintf("\n- **AUC = %.3f** \u2014 ", auc_val))
  • AUC = 0.517 <U+2014>
if (auc_val < 0.55) {
  cat("near 0.5, indicating the model has no meaningful discriminatory power.\n")
} else if (auc_val < 0.7) {
  cat("indicating weak discriminatory power.\n")
} else {
  cat("indicating moderate to good discriminatory power.\n")
}

near 0.5, indicating the model has no meaningful discriminatory power.

cat(sprintf("- **Accuracy = %.1f%%** on the held-out test set.\n\n", acc * 100))
  • Accuracy = 50.6% on the held-out test set.
cat("**Limitations:**\n\n")

Limitations:

cat("- **Cross-sectional design:** The outcome (Fatigue below vs. above median) ",
    "is not a measure of change over time. The assignment asks for a model of ",
    "whether an outcome decreases after medication; however, the symptom data ",
    "provides only a single observation per patient with no pre-treatment baseline. ",
    "A genuine before/after analysis would require longitudinal symptom records.\n")
  • Cross-sectional design: The outcome (Fatigue below vs. above median) is not a measure of change over time. The assignment asks for a model of whether an outcome decreases after medication; however, the symptom data provides only a single observation per patient with no pre-treatment baseline. A genuine before/after analysis would require longitudinal symptom records.
cat("- **Observational data:** Patients were not randomly assigned to therapies, ",
    "so any associations may be confounded by indication.\n")
  • Observational data: Patients were not randomly assigned to therapies, so any associations may be confounded by indication.
cat("- **Simulated data:** All patterns (or lack thereof) reflect Synthea's ",
    "data-generating process rather than real clinical relationships.\n")
  • Simulated data: All patterns (or lack thereof) reflect Synthea’s data-generating process rather than real clinical relationships.
